healthy_apple:
🍎 Healthy apple detected!

Findings:
Your apple looks healthy with no visible disease signs.

Care recommendations:
• Continue regular watering (2–3 times per week)
• Apply organic fertilizer every 4–6 weeks
• Prune dry branches preventively
• Watch for pests
• Harvest on time for best quality
apple_scab:
🍂 Apple scab detected!

Findings:
Scab is a fungal disease affecting apple leaves and fruit.

Care recommendations:
• Remove and destroy affected leaves and fruit
• Treat with fungicides (Bordeaux mixture, copper sulfate) per local regulations
• Spray early spring before bud break
• Remove all fallen leaves in autumn
• Thin the canopy for better airflow

Prevention:
• Choose resistant varieties
• Schedule preventive sprays
• Keep the trunk circle clean
black_rot:
🖤 Black rot detected!

Findings:
Black rot is a serious fungal fruit disease.

Care recommendations:
• Remove affected fruit immediately
• Apply fungicides per label
• Improve air circulation around the tree
• Avoid fruit damage during care
• Handle harvest gently

Prevention:
• Regular fungicide program in season
• Control humidity
• Timely harvest
cedar_apple_rust:
🧡 Cedar-apple rust detected!

Findings:
A fungal disease requiring two hosts (apple and juniper).

Care recommendations:
• Remove nearby junipers if possible
• Use sulfur-containing fungicides
• Remove affected leaves
• Spray in spring before bloom

Prevention:
• Plant apples away from junipers
• Regular tree inspection
powdery_mildew:
⚪ Powdery mildew detected!

Findings:
A fungal disease with a white powdery coating.

Care recommendations:
• Treat with baking soda solution (1 tbsp per 1 L water)
• Use sulfur products
• Remove heavily affected shoots
• Improve canopy ventilation

Prevention:
• Avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer
• Follow proper watering
• Preventive sprays as needed
fire_blight:
🔥 Fire blight detected!

Findings:
A serious bacterial disease requiring prompt action.

Care recommendations:
• Urgently remove affected branches (20–30 cm below damage)
• Disinfect tools after each cut
• Use plant antibiotics where legally allowed
• Severe cases may require tree removal

Prevention:
• Control insect pollinators as vectors
• Avoid pruning in wet weather
• Choose resistant varieties
healthy_leaf:
🌿 Healthy apple leaf!

Findings:
Leaves look healthy with no disease signs.

Care recommendations:
• Continue current care
• Inspect the tree regularly
• Maintain optimal watering
• Apply balanced fertilizer
• Prune on schedule
